#include "std/string"
#include "std/set"
#include "std/array"

def solve(points : Array(Array(Int64)), target : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  
  # Helper function to create a unique string key for a point [x, y, z]
  def point_to_key(point : Array(Int64)) : String
    point[0].to_s + "," + point[1].to_s + "," + point[2].to_s
  end

  target_key = point_to_key(target)

  # P stores all unique point keys found so far.
  P = Set(String)
  # L stores the actual coordinate arrays of all unique points found so far.
  L = Array(Array(Int64)).new

  # --- Initialization (k=0) ---
  initial_points_set = Set(String)
  points.each do |p|
    p_key = point_to_key(p)
    if !initial_points_set.include?(p_key)
      initial_points_set.add(p_key)
      P.add(p_key)
      L.push(p)
    end
  end

  if P.include?(target_key)
    return 0
  end

  # Cannot generate new points if less than 2 unique points exist.
  if L.size < 2
    return -1
  end

  # --- Iterative Generation (k >= 1) ---
  k = 1
  
  loop do
    # Points available at the beginning of generation k
    L_prev = L.dup 
    generated_new_points = Array(Array(Int64)).new
    new_points_found_in_generation = false
    
    N_prev = L_prev.size
    
    # Generate points for generation k using pairs from L_prev
    # Iterate over all distinct pairs of points (i < j)
    (0...N_prev).each do |i|
      (i + 1...N_prev).each do |j|
        p1 = L_prev[i]
        p2 = L_prev[j]
        
        # Calculate midpoint c = [floor((x1 + x2) / 2), ...]
        # Since coordinates are non-negative, integer division truncates towards zero, 
        # which is equivalent to floor.
        new_x = (p1[0] + p2[0]) / 2
        new_y = (p1[1] + p2[1]) / 2
        new_z = (p1[2] + p2[2]) / 2
        
        new_point = [new_x, new_y, new_z]
        new_point_key = point_to_key(new_point)
        
        # Check if this point is truly new to the entire set P
        if !P.include?(new_point_key)
          new_points_found_in_generation = true
          
          # Check if this newly generated point is the target
          if new_point_key == target_key
            return k
          end
          
          # Collect the point for updating L and P later
          generated_new_points.push(new_point)
        end
      end
    end
    
    if !new_points_found_in_generation
      # No new unique points were generated.
      return -1
    end
    
    # Update the state: P and L include the newly generated points
    generated_new_points.each do |p|
      p_key = point_to_key(p)
      P.add(p_key)
      L.push(p)
    end

    k += 1
    
    # Maximum possible unique points is 7^3 = 343. Stop if we exceed this.
    if L.size > 343 
      return -1
    end
  end
end
```
